sources.name: NOAA ENC Direct
sources.notes: Official NOAA ENC Direct layers expose charted wrecks and obstructions through ArcGIS REST services.

sources.name: NOAA Historical Hurricane Tracks
sources.notes: Storm path and intensity history for correlating loss events with track corridors.

sources.name: Library of Congress Chronicling America
sources.notes: Searchable newspaper archive for storm, salvage, and cargo-loss references.

sources.name: Florida Memory
sources.notes: Florida state archive for maritime history, newspapers, maps, and local records.

knownWrecks.name: 1715 Plate Fleet Scatter
knownWrecks.cargoProfile: silver coins, bullion, and fleet cargo
knownWrecks.historicalNotes: High-value Spanish fleet losses concentrated along the Treasure Coast after a hurricane landfall.

knownWrecks.name: HMS Fowey
knownWrecks.historicalNotes: Useful as a reef-navigation analog near Biscayne approaches, even if treasure profile is modest.

knownWrecks.name: SS Copenhagen
knownWrecks.historicalNotes: Modern wreck showing continued hazard clustering over the same reef lines.

knownWrecks.name: El Salvador / Gil Blas Loss Corridor
knownWrecks.cargoProfile: merchant cargo with scattered specie reports
knownWrecks.historicalNotes: Representative high-energy loss corridor tied to shoals and Gulf Stream set around Cape Canaveral.

knownWrecks.name: Northern St. Augustine Loss Cluster
knownWrecks.cargoProfile: colonial supplies, artillery, and settlement stores
knownWrecks.historicalNotes: Early colonial losses concentrated near inlet approaches and shifting bars.

stormEvents.name: 1565 Colonial Hurricane
stormEvents.notes: Storm conditions tied to early colonial settlement and inlet losses.

stormEvents.name: 1715 Treasure Coast Hurricane
stormEvents.notes: Anchor event for bullion-bearing fleet losses along the east-central coast.

stormEvents.name: Mid-Century Cape Storm Corridor
stormEvents.notes: Used for shoal and current compounding around Cape Canaveral.

stormEvents.name: Second Seminole War Era Gale Season
stormEvents.notes: Repeated gales raised loss probability for smaller coastal traffic.

stormEvents.name: Great Miami Hurricane
stormEvents.notes: Modern comparison case for extreme energy on shallow reef and inlet systems.

journalEntries.title: Fleet driven onto the coast
journalEntries.excerpt: Paraphrase: reports describe multiple ships driven ashore after a hurricane while sailing north with heavy treasure cargo.

journalEntries.title: Shoals and current off the cape
journalEntries.excerpt: Paraphrase: mariners note strong set toward shoals near the cape during foul weather and poor visibility.

journalEntries.title: Bar crossing loss risk near St. Augustine
journalEntries.excerpt: Paraphrase: coastal bars and sudden storms were repeatedly cited as causes of vessel loss near the settlement approaches.

journalEntries.title: Wrecking activity south of Jupiter
journalEntries.excerpt: Paraphrase: coastal newspapers describe repeated strandings and salvage activity after seasonal gales.

journalEntries.title: Reef hazards near Biscayne
journalEntries.excerpt: Paraphrase: ship accounts emphasize poor charts, reef strikes, and scattered salvage near Biscayne approaches.

candidateZones.name: Sebastian Ledge Corridor
candidateZones.searchNote: Broad corridor where storm-driven fleet debris, reef impact, and littoral drift repeatedly overlap.

candidateZones.name: Cape Canaveral Shoal Shelf
candidateZones.searchNote: High-energy cape corridor with current set, shoal interaction, and fewer headline treasure recoveries than the Treasure Coast.

candidateZones.name: St. Augustine Bar Approaches
candidateZones.searchNote: Inlet bars and shifting sands likely hide early colonial loss signatures beneath later sediment.

candidateZones.name: Jupiter to Palm Beach Breakers
candidateZones.searchNote: Less famous than Sebastian but structurally similar for storm-driven coastal trade losses.

candidateZones.name: Biscayne Outer Reef Line
candidateZones.searchNote: Heavy known navigation hazard zone with likely documentation density but lower undiscovered high-value probability.

candidateZones.name: Fort Pierce Shoal Cluster
candidateZones.searchNote: Treasure Coast extension with charted wreck density and lower public attention than the Sebastian cluster.

candidateZones.name: Cocoa Reef Transit Line
candidateZones.searchNote: Space Coast transit lane where storm tracks, shallows, and lesser-known charted hazards converge.
